messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Leaking build processes when marking a session as done
ISSUE BODY: * Create a new AHP Copilot CLI session
* Use it ...
* Mark as done
* Observe the processes are leaking:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3e91c97e-6ecd-4ed8-b881-d8d5db683133

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### `node/copilotcliSession.ts`  
**CopilotCLISession**
- Wraps a single `Session` object from the `@github/copilot/sdk`
- Entry point for every chat request via `handleRequest()`
- Listens to SDK events and translates them to VS Code chat UI parts
- Manages permission flow
- Tracks external edits via `ExternalEditTracker` for proper diff display
- Supports CLI commands: `compact`, `plan`, `fleet`
- Built-in slash commands: `/commit`, `/sync`, `/merge`, `/create-pr`, `/create-draft-pr`, `/update-pr`
- Captures pull request URLs from `create_pull_request` tool results

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Copilot CLI (Terminal Session)  
Terminal CLI sessions ("New Copilot CLI Session") run as a separate process. When OTel is enabled, the extension forwards `COPILOT_OTEL_ENABLED` and `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` to the terminal process. Terminal traces appear as **independent root traces** (service `github-copilot`) — they are not linked to extension traces.  
> **Note:** The CLI runtime only supports `otlp-http`. When `otlp-grpc` is configured, the terminal CLI still uses HTTP. Backends that serve both protocols on the same port (e.g., Aspire Dashboard) work transparently.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
#### session_start — session metadata (appears once at session start)
```jsonl
{"ts":1773200251300,"dur":0,"sid":"62f52dec","type":"session_start","name":"session_start","spanId":"session-start-62f52dec","status":"ok","attrs":{"copilotVersion":"0.43.2026033104","vscodeVersion":"1.99.0"}}
```
Key attrs: `copilotVersion`, `vscodeVersion`. Useful for identifying which build produced the logs.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
--- name: chronicle:standup description: Generate a standup report from recent chat sessions
---
Generate a standup report from my recent coding sessions. Use the **chronicle** skill — it documents the `copilot_sessionStoreSql` tool, the session-store schema, and the Standup workflow for summarizing the last 24h of activity from `sessions`, `session_refs`, `turns`, and `session_files`.  
When you invoke `copilot_sessionStoreSql`, set `subcommand: "standup"` on every call.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\assets\prompts\chronicle-tips.prompt.md =====  
--- name: chronicle:tips description: Get personalized tips based on your chat session usage patterns
---
Analyze my recent chat session history and give me personalized tips to improve my workflow. Use the **chronicle** skill — it documents the `copilot_sessionStoreSql` tool, the session-store schema, and the Tips workflow for investigating usage patterns from `sessions`, `turns`, `session_files`, and `session_refs`.  
When you invoke `copilot_sessionStoreSql`, set `subcommand: "tips"` on every call.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\assets\prompts\plan.prompt.md =====  
--- name: plan description: Research and plan with the Plan agent agent: Plan argument-hint: Describe what you want to plan or research
---
Plan my task.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\chat-lib\README.md =====

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Dependencies  
- `@github/copilot/sdk`: Official Copilot CLI SDK (session management, tools, permissions, events)

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix leaking build processes when marking a Copilot CLI session as done",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["bug", "performance", "cli-session"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a developer using the Copilot CLI extension, I want the terminal process to properly terminate when marking a session as done so that system resources are not leaked and background processes don't continue running unnecessarily.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a Copilot CLI session is active in the terminal",
    "When the user clicks 'Mark as Done' button",
    "Then the underlying CLI process should terminate within 5 seconds"
  ],
  "story_points": 8,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate CopilotCLISession class lifecycle methods for proper cleanup",
      "Add session termination handler that calls process.kill() or equivalent",
      "Implement graceful shutdown sequence (flush logs, close connections)",
      "Add error handling for process termination failures"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Verify 'Mark as Done' button triggers correct API endpoint/command",
      "Ensure UI feedback shows session is closing/terminated"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create test case: Start CLI session, mark as done, verify process exits within 5 seconds",
      "Test case: Verify no zombie processes remain after session completion",
      "Regression test: Existing sessions still work correctly"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Process termination code implemented and tested",
    "All existing tests pass",
    "No memory/process leaks in CI/CD pipeline",
    "Code reviewed by senior engineer"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What is the exact API endpoint or command that 'Mark as Done' invokes?",
    "Does CopilotCLISession have an existing close/terminate method we should extend?",
    "Are there any SDK events for session completion that we need to handle?",
    "Is this a terminal process or a separate spawned process?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The CLI process is spawned as a child process by the extension",
    "There should be a cleanup method in CopilotCLISession class",
    "Process termination can be done via standard OS kill mechanism"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "node/copilotcliSession.ts - Session management implementation",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Copilot CLI documentation and session metadata"
  ]
}
```
